evals.prompt: Create an infographic for Twitter showing the top 5 programming languages in 2025 by usage: Python 29.8%, JavaScript 24.5%, TypeScript 12.1%, Java 9.7%, Rust 4.2%. Make it bold and modern — I want it to pop in a feed.
evals.expected_output: A Twitter-sized (1200px wide) infographic as HTML + PNG showing the 5 languages with their percentages. Bold, distinctive design with horizontal bars or visual elements for each language. Phosphor icons used (no emojis). All 5 languages and all 5 percentages visible.
expectations: An HTML file is produced containing the infographic
All five programming languages are present: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Rust
All five percentage values are present: 29.8%, 24.5%, 12.1%, 9.7%, 4.2%
The design uses a distinctive display font (not Inter, Roboto, or Arial)
Phosphor Icons CDN script is included — no emojis used as UI icons
The infographic width is set to 1200px (Twitter platform size) or explicitly uses Twitter dimensions
A PNG file is produced

evals.prompt: Make a one-pager infographic explaining how our SaaS product 'Dataflow' works. Our brand color is #00C9A7. Steps: 1) Sign up & verify email, 2) Connect your data sources (CSV, API, or database), 3) Our AI analyzes and structures your data automatically, 4) Customize your dashboard with drag-and-drop, 5) Invite your team & set permissions. Keep it clean and professional — this is for our website.
evals.expected_output: A branded how-it-works infographic featuring the company name 'Dataflow', the brand teal color #00C9A7 (or close), and all 5 numbered steps. Clean, professional look for website use.
expectations: An HTML file is produced containing the infographic
The company name 'Dataflow' appears prominently in the infographic
The brand color #00C9A7 (or a close teal approximation) is used in the design
All five steps are present with their content (sign up, connect data sources, AI analysis, dashboard customization, invite team)
The steps are presented as a numbered sequence (1 through 5)
The design has a light or neutral background (not dark mode) appropriate for a website
A PNG file is produced

evals.prompt: I need an infographic comparing remote work vs in-office work. Data: Remote — 22% higher self-reported productivity, saves avg 4.5 hrs/week commuting, 67% report collaboration challenges, 38% feel isolated. In-office — 31% more impromptu collaboration, 44% report more distractions, 28% lower stress from commuting. Use a light, editorial look. No dark background please.
evals.expected_output: A side-by-side comparison infographic with two columns (Remote vs In-Office). Light background. All key statistics present: 22%, 4.5 hrs, 67%, 38%, 31%, 44%, 28%. Clean editorial aesthetic.
expectations: An HTML file is produced containing the infographic
The infographic has a two-column or side-by-side structure contrasting remote and in-office work
All seven statistics are present: 22%, 4.5 hrs, 67%, 38%, 31%, 44%, 28%
The background is light (white, off-white, or light gray) — not dark
Both 'Remote' and 'In-Office' (or equivalent labels) are clearly labeled as separate sections
A PNG file is produced
